message:
feat(runtime_compat): tick 162 Command::output_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Thirteenth post-storage tick. Closes the last deferred
runtime_compat target from tick 160. runtime_compat.rs now has
cx-first coverage for every real async fn where cx threading
provides value.

New sibling:
- process::Command::output_with_cx — bridges cx cancellation into
  the existing `cancel: Arc<AtomicBool>` signal that
  `run_output_command` polls at PROCESS_POLL_INTERVAL (10ms).
  A dedicated watcher task polls `cx.is_cancel_requested()` at
  the same interval; when cancellation is detected it sets the
  shared AtomicBool, which the blocking worker observes within
  one tick and surfaces as
  `io::Error::new(ErrorKind::Interrupted, "process command cancelled")`.

Integration with kill_on_drop: kill-on-drop semantics are
preserved — if the future is dropped before completion and
kill_on_drop was set, the KillOnDropGuard still fires the cancel
flag. The cx watcher sets `watcher_done` on normal-path exit so
it never leaks past the body.

Integration with spawn_blocking: the existing body is unchanged;
the cx-first variant only adds the bridge watcher + pre-flight
checkpoint. Legacy `output` preserved for callers on the
ambient-cx path.

Test: `process_command_output_with_cx_cancellation_surfaces_as_interrupted`
— spawns a `sleep 10` child, then a short-lived task cancels
the cx after 100ms. Asserts:
1. The returned IO error is `ErrorKind::Interrupted`.
2. Elapsed time < 2s (the 10s child sleep would dominate if cx
   cancel were ignored).

If the watcher were broken (never setting the AtomicBool), the
test would exceed the 2s budget and fail with a clear elapsed-time
assertion message. Verified via fork bypass: 194/194 runtime_compat::
tests pass (+1 from 193).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
